ocr.text: The data presented in the table provides valuable insights into the characteristics and behavioral responses of hybridoma cells
derived from normal mice. Notably, the column labeled "Leptin (ng/mL)" indicates the concentration of this crucial hormone,
which is often associated with appetite regulation and energy balance. The variations in leptin levels among different
transformed cell designations, such as G6g1 and E4e1, highlight the potential influence of genetic backgrounds on leptin
expression. Furthermore, the metrics of "Odour Intensity" and "Hedonic Tone" offer a nuanced understanding of the sensory
experiences associated with these hybridomas, shedding light on how these cells may respond to various environmental stimuli.
Leptin (ng/mL)
Normal mice  18.84 ± 1.36###
HFD-fed mice
+efaroxan and allantoin  61.86 ± 2.25***
Fusion  Growth-arrest after fusion  Growth stimulatory antigen or cytokine  Transformed cell (designation)  Characteristics of hybridoma (reference)  Parental cell

YACUT × 20MLC  Yes (rapid cell death without stimulation)  DBA/2 splenocytes  3A2, 7D4  Non-MHC-restricted cytotoxicity (65). Activation and inhibitory receptors+ (66, 67)  CTL
YACUT × 20MLC  Yes  DBA/2 splenocytes  B6HO3  IFN-γ production in response to IL-18 produced by dying bacteria-infected macrophages (68, 69)  Innate-like αβ T cell
YACUT × 20MLC  Yes  Irradiated splenocytes  CB1  Requirement of TNF-α for the growth in the presence of irradiated splenocytes CD3−, TCR−  Innate lymphoid cell?
YACUT × iNKT  Yes  IL-7 + IL-15  F3a  Growth in longitudinal cell clusters; CD244.1 and 2+  iNKT cell

Odour Intensity (8-3-3-1 tanh)  Hedonic Tone (8-3-2-1 Leaky ReLUs)
The findings suggest that splenocytes exhibiting Mlsa, H-2b, or H-2d antigens can significantly affect both the growth-arrest
mechanisms post-fusion and the subsequent growth stimulatory activities, thereby influencing the overall functional properties
of the hybridoma. This information underscores the importance of genetic and immunological factors in the development of
hybridoma technologies.
